text: A dogfooding/mechanics test of the openreview systematic-review pipeline (the "HAPI path"), run repeatedly against a deliberately tiny dataset to exercise the full flow end to end. The "anger stored in fat cells" study was deleted up front, and after some back-and-forth over whether to minimize data, the run settled on a small melatonin-for-jet-lag literature review as the throwaway test case; the study was created, run through protocol â†’ PubMed search â†’ dual-independent title/abstract and full-text screening â†’ extraction â†’ evaluation â†’ views/report, then deleted between iterations so core-engine changes could be re-tested cleanly. A realism note surfaced that only abstracts were retrievable for the older paywalled included studies, so full-text screening ran on abstracts by design. The session closed by producing a self-contained, copy-paste research request (question "does oral melatonin reduce jet lag?", slug melatonin-jet-lag, single PubMed query ~14 records, full PICO, inclusion/exclusion reasons, and per-arm extraction fields) for the user to hand to a fresh session to reproduce the exact minimal run.
